title: Eduversal Teacher Appraisal Level Definitions v2.0
description: Defines the four appraisal levels, their entry criteria, item set sizes, and scoring logic. A teacher's level is determined jointly by their Eduversal subject leader (zümre başkanı) and the partner school principal at the beginning of each academic year. Level assignment is recorded in the teacher's profile and governs which items appear in their formal appraisal.

level_determination.timing: Start of each academic year (July), recorded before appraisal observations begin

levels.label: Induction
levels.description: Teachers in their first or second year of service, or teachers new to the Eduversal network regardless of prior experience, are placed at the Induction level. The appraisal at this level focuses on establishing foundational professional competencies. Expectations are calibrated to support rather than evaluate against full professional mastery.
levels.typical_profile: Year 1–2 of teaching service; no prior Eduversal appraisal score on record

entry_criteria.primary: Years of service ≤ 2 years in the Eduversal network
entry_criteria.override: May be assigned by Subject Leader / Principal if a teacher is new to the Eduversal context regardless of total years taught elsewhere

scoring.f2_mean_note: F2 mean is the simple mean of all scored D1 + D2 + D3 + D4 items visible at this level
scoring.output: Score expressed as percentage (0–100); predicate assigned per predicate_bands table

support_provisions: Assigned a mentor (Subject Leader) who conducts monthly walkthrough observations from Month 2 onwards
Mentor debrief after each walkthrough — narrative feedback using walkthrough-rubric.json
Formal Appraisal conducted once per year (Term 2 or 3) by Subject Leader + one external appraiser
Induction stages: Stage 0 (June preparation), Stage 1 (July orientation), Stage 2 (July–Sept foundation), Stage 3 (Oct–May anchoring)

levels.label: Developing
levels.description: Teachers who have completed their induction period but have not yet achieved a Proficient predicate (B or above) in their most recent formal appraisal. The Developing level introduces a broader item set — adding higher-order thinking, teaching strategy variety, values integration, and fuller work inspection responsibilities.
levels.typical_profile: Year 3–5 in service; or most recent appraisal predicate C, D, or F

entry_criteria.primary: Years of service 3–5 AND/OR most recent appraisal predicate in range C–F
entry_criteria.note: A teacher who completed induction with predicate A or B may be placed directly at Proficient at the discretion of the Subject Leader and Principal

scoring.f2_mean_note: F2 mean is the simple mean of all scored D1 + D2 + D3 + D4 items visible at this level
scoring.output: Score expressed as percentage (0–100); predicate assigned per predicate_bands table

support_provisions: Walkthrough observations at least once per term (3 per year minimum)
Formal Appraisal conducted once per year
Eligible for targeted professional development based on weakest framework area

levels.label: Proficient
levels.description: Teachers with a sustained track record of competent practice — typically 5 or more years of service and a most recent appraisal predicate of B or above. The Proficient level adds the full D3 instructional range (except the Lead-only item), full D4 assessment items, and expects comprehensive professional engagement.
levels.typical_profile: 5+ years in service; most recent appraisal predicate B or A

entry_criteria.primary: Years of service ≥ 5 AND most recent appraisal predicate B (66–85) or A (86–100)
entry_criteria.alternative: Outstanding performance at Developing level (predicate A) may accelerate placement at Subject Leader recommendation

scoring.f2_mean_note: F2 mean is the simple mean of all scored D1 + D2 + D3 + D4 items visible at this level
scoring.output: Score expressed as percentage (0–100); predicate assigned per predicate_bands table

support_provisions: Walkthrough observations at least twice per year
Formal Appraisal conducted once per year
Eligible to serve as peer observer for Induction or Developing level colleagues
Encouraged to pursue Cambridge or Marzano professional credentials

levels.label: Lead
levels.description: Subject Leaders, Heads of Department, or teachers formally designated with pedagogical leadership responsibilities across the Eduversal network. The Lead level assesses all 64 standard items plus 6 additional leadership items (F3L). The full D3 instructional range applies including D3.21 (Determination and Persistence).
levels.typical_profile: Subject Leader or HoD designation; typically 5+ years with sustained predicate A or B; formal leadership title assigned by Eduversal

entry_criteria.primary: Formal designation as Subject Leader (zümre başkanı) or Head of Department by Eduversal HQ
entry_criteria.secondary: May include teachers assigned network-wide pedagogical leadership responsibilities even without HoD title

scoring.f3_combined_note: F3 combined mean = simple mean of all F3 items (F3.1–F3.10) + all F3L items (L1–L6) — 16 items total
scoring.f2_mean_note: F2 mean is the simple mean of all D1 + D2 + D3 + D4 items (including D3.21)
scoring.output: Score expressed as percentage (0–100); predicate assigned per predicate_bands table

support_provisions: Formal Appraisal conducted once per year with a two-appraiser panel (Eduversal Representative + Partner School Principal)
Pre-appraisal self-evaluation required using the Lead self-assessment tool
Post-appraisal leadership development plan produced jointly with Eduversal zümre başkanı
Eligible for recognition in Eduversal Educator of the Year programme (predicate A only)

appraisal_calendar.level_assignment: July — first two weeks of academic year

walkthrough_cycle.lead: Minimum 2 per academic year (one by Eduversal representative)

appraisal_calendar.formal_appraisal_window: Term 2 preferred (January–February); may be conducted Term 3 by exception
appraisal_calendar.teacher_acknowledgement_deadline: One week after report is shared with teacher
